solute.description: This molecule is a diaryl sulfonamide consisting of a central sulfonamide bridge (–SO₂NH–) that links a phenyl ring to a 4-chlorophenyl ring. The primary functional groups are the sulfonamide moiety and a chlorine atom substituted at the para position of one aromatic ring. It is a medium-sized, polar molecule characterized by a hydrogen bond donor at the nitrogen atom and two hydrogen bond acceptors at the sulfonyl oxygens. The structure is defined by its rigid aromatic framework and the electronic influence of the electronegative chlorine substituent on the N-phenyl ring.

solvent.description: This molecule is a linear, six-carbon saturated hydrocarbon chain with no branching or cyclic components. It lacks functional groups, consisting entirely of single carbon-carbon and carbon-hydrogen bonds. The structure is strictly non-polar and possesses no hydrogen bond donors or acceptors. As a medium-length aliphatic alkane, its character is defined solely by its hydrophobic backbone and lack of electronic complexity.
